subject: Physics
title: Benjamin Franklin's Kite
description: Learn Benjamin Franklin's Kite in one minute.

hook.text: In 1752, Benjamin Franklin flew a kite in a thunderstorm and proved that lightning is electrical.

buildup.text: He attached a metal key to the kite string and waited in a shed as storm clouds gathered.

discovery.text: Loose fibers on the string stood on end. When he reached toward the key, a spark jumped—electricity.

twist.text: The next person to try the experiment—Georg Richmann in Russia—was killed by the electrical discharge.

climax.text: Franklin's experiment led directly to the lightning rod, which has saved countless buildings and lives.

punchline.text: A kite, a key, and a thunderstorm. Franklin tamed lightning.

quiz.question: What practical invention resulted from Franklin's kite experiment?
